name: Pozo do Inferno and Casón Waterfalls Trail
name_original: Feas – sendero de Cascada de Casón
description: Hike the moderate 9.6-mile Feas – Cascada de Casón trail to discover two waterfalls and panoramic views from Casón Castle ruins.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Ortigueira-Mera traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive estuaries, rugged coastlines, and inland woodlands. The region features significant natural elements such as the Ría de Ortigueira and the Mera River, alongside mountains like A Faladoira. Hikers encounter varied terrain, from sandy stretches and dramatic cliffs to riverine ecosystems and picturesque waterfalls. This Special Conservation Zone offers a range of natural features suitable for accessible outdoor exploration. Best easy hiking trails in Ortigueira-Mera The most popular easy hiking route is Casón Waterfall – Pozo do Inferno Waterfall loop from A Pena do Vilar, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete, leading through forested areas to two notable waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alley with a Sea View – Viewpoint loop from Espasante, an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) path. This route offers coastal views around the village of Espasante. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of San Julián loop from Cariño, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through coastal paths, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking in Ortigueira-Mera is defined by coastal paths, extensive estuaries, and native woodlands, with options for various ability levels. The routes in Ortigueira-Mera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Ortigueira-Mera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Ortigueira-Mera?
A: Ortigueira-Mera offers a good selection of easy hiking trails, with over 20 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, the region features more than 50 hiking routes across various difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on easy hikes in Ortigueira-Mera?
A: Easy hikes in Ortigueira-Mera traverse diverse landscapes, including extensive estuaries like the Ría de Ortigueira, rugged coastlines with sandy stretches and cliffs, and inland areas with eucalyptus and native woodlands. You'll find paths along rivers, through forests, and offering panoramic coastal views.

Q: Are there any easy trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can explore easy trails that lead to picturesque waterfalls. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casón Waterfall – Pozo do Inferno Waterfall loop from A Pena do Vilar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) route that takes you through forested areas to both Cascada de Casón and Pozo do Inferno waterfalls. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40338468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casón Waterfall – Pozo do Inferno Waterfall loop from Fervenza do Casón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which easy hikes offer the best coastal views or viewpoints?
A: For stunning coastal views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17318847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alley with a Sea View – Viewpoint loop from Espasante&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.1-mile (3.4 km) path offering scenic vistas around the village of Espasante. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40339867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Julián loop from Cariño&lt;/a&gt;, which follows coastal paths with expansive views.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Ortigueira-Mera suitable for families with children?
A: Many easy trails in Ortigueira-Mera are suitable for families. Routes that are shorter in distance and have minimal elevation gain, such as the waterfall loops or coastal walks, are generally good choices. The diverse scenery, from beaches to forests, provides engaging experiences for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hikes in Ortigueira-Mera?
A: Yes, Ortigueira-Mera is generally dog-friendly. Most easy hiking trails allow dogs, especially those in natural areas. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to clean up after them.

Q: Are there many circular easy walks available?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails in Ortigueira-Mera are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casón Waterfall – Pozo do Inferno Waterfall loop from A Pena do Vilar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17318847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alley with a Sea View – Viewpoint loop from Espasante&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for easy hikes in this region?
A: Easy hikes in Ortigueira-Mera typically range from 2 to 4 miles (3 to 6 km) in distance. The duration can vary, but most can be completed within 1 to 2 hours, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40339867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Julián loop from Cariño&lt;/a&gt; is 3.7 miles (6.0 km) and takes about 1 hour 43 minutes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go on easy hikes in Ortigueira-Mera?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Ortigueira-Mera is generally during spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially for coastal walks, but it can be warmer and more crowded.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on easy trails?
A: Absolutely. Ortigueira-Mera is a Special Conservation Zone, particularly known for its birdlife, especially around the estuaries. You might spot migratory aquatic birds, including Eurasian curlews, and various riverine fauna like dippers and herons along the Mera River. The diverse ecosystems provide habitats for a range of species.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can be found near easy hiking trails?
A: The region has a rich heritage. Ortigueira-Mera is part of the Camino de Santiago, offering a blend of natural exploration and cultural discovery. The town of Ortigueira itself features historical architecture and the Museo Ortigalia houses Galicia's oldest dolmen. Coastal trails might also pass by ancient hermitage sites or fishing villages.

Q: Are there any easy trails that lead to beaches or potential wild swimming spots?
A: Yes, the coastal nature of Ortigueira-Mera means many easy trails offer access to beaches and coves. Morouzos beach is a notable sandy stretch. While specific designated wild swimming spots aren't highlighted, the numerous estuaries and coastal areas provide opportunities for enjoying the water, especially during warmer months.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Ortigueira-Mera?
A: The easy trails in Ortigueira-Mera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.8 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse scenery, from dramatic coastlines to serene woodlands and beautiful waterfalls, as well as the well-maintained paths suitable for all abilities.
tips: Go down a small path until you reach the Pozo do Inferno Waterfall. You can get very close to the waterfall using the small wooden bridge. Safe route for the whole family
The Penido Major peak at 550 m above sea level offers spectacular views towards the Ortigueira estuary

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Feas – Cascada de Casón trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. While some sections are easily accessible, good fitness and sure-footedness are recommended, especially for longer or more challenging parts with elevation gain. There are also options for shorter, easier loops suitable for families.

faqs.question: What kind of route is the Feas – Cascada de Casón trail?
faqs.answer: The route is described as a "double hiking route" with a bifurcation at the start. One path leads towards the Pozo do Inferno Waterfall, and the other towards the Castelo do Casón ruins. This allows for flexibility, including options for shorter loops.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the Feas – Cascada de Casón trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in Feás, in the municipality of Cariño. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route includes forest tracks wide enough for vehicles in some sections, suggesting accessible starting points. Look for designated parking near the trail's beginning in Feás.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features two splendid waterfalls: the Casón Waterfall and the Pozo do Inferno Waterfall. You'll also find a natural viewpoint at the ruins of Casón Castle, offering incredible panoramic views of the entire Ría de Ortigueira, stretching from Estaca de Bares to Cariño.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain consists mostly of accessible paths, including forest tracks that are wide enough for vehicles in some sections. You'll encounter a wooden bridge crossing the Rego do Casón near the Casón Waterfall and a small wooden walkway at the Pozo do Inferno Waterfall for close-up views.

faqs.question: Is the Feas – Cascada de Casón trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially around waterfalls, viewpoints, and other hikers, to ensure safety and respect for the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the trail?
faqs.answer: The waterfalls are often at their most impressive after periods of rain or during spring when water levels are higher. Visiting during spring or autumn can also offer pleasant temperatures for hiking and beautiful natural scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter might be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Feas – Cascada de Casón trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Bike Norte and the Enlace S Andres- Bike Norte trails.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: For the most rewarding views, make sure to ascend to the ruins of Casón Castle. The path involves climbing to about 340 meters in altitude, but the panoramic views of the Ría de Ortigueira from this historical viewpoint are truly spectacular and worth the effort.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the full route is moderate, there are options for shorter, easier loops that are suitable for families with children. These sections allow younger hikers to enjoy the natural beauty without committing to the entire distance or elevation gain.
